page.content: In the analysis of biomarkers, the table presents data categorized into two primary sections: PCT (μg/L) and hsCRP (mg/L), detailing various thresholds with corresponding metrics.

lines.text: In the analysis of biomarkers, the table presents data categorized into two primary sections: PCT (μg/L) and hsCRP (mg/L),
detailing various thresholds with corresponding metrics.

page.content: For PCT, the thresholds and respective values are as follows: for PCT > 0.1, the sample size is 406 (75%), with sensitivity at 0.90, specificity at 0.59, likelihood ratio positive (LR +) at 2.22, and likelihood ratio negative (LR -) at 0.16; for PCT > 0.25, the sample size is 300 (55%), sensitivity is 0.74, specificity is 0.85, LR + is 4.87, and LR - is 0.31; for PCT > 0.5, the sample size is 225 (41%), sensitivity is 0.57, specificity is 0.93, LR + is 8.21, and LR - is 0.46; finally, for PCT > 1.0, the sample size is 167 (31%), with sensitivity at 0.43, specificity at 0.96, LR + at 10.57, and LR - at 0.59. The hsCRP analysis shows: for hsCRP > 40, the sample size is 413 (76%), sensitivity is 0.89, specificity is 0.52, LR + is 1.86, and LR - is 0.22; for hsCRP > 50, the sample size is 384 (70%), sensitivity is 0.87, specificity is 0.65, LR + is 2.44, and LR - is 0.21; for hsCRP > 100, the sample size is 281 (52%), sensitivity is 0.69, specificity is 0.86, LR + is 4.94, and LR - is 0.36; for hsCRP > 200, the sample size is 141 (26%), sensitivity is 0.36, specificity is 0.96, LR + is 8.83, and LR - is 0.67.
